title: Fix hyperbolic unit test
body:
The `1x-T4-4-17` instance type were missing in the latest catalog (commit 06cb4087855609446f3ce9739d863ffd92cdbcd6), causing this unit test to fail, as it's explicitly looking for that type.

This PR fixes it by switching to a valid instance type which exist on the latest catalog.

We probably should still investigate though why `1x-T4-4-17` went missing.

Tested:
- formatting
- relevant individual tests

Not run:
- full smoke tests
- backward compat tests

step_plan.summary: Fixes a failing Hyperbolic unit test by replacing a hard-coded, now-missing instance type (`1x-T4-4-17`) with an instance type that exists in the latest Hyperbolic catalog, ideally chosen in a more stable/catalog-driven way.

steps.operation: Update the Hyperbolic feasibility unit test to use a currently valid instance type (preferably selected dynamically from the Hyperbolic catalog, falling back to a known-good hard-coded type) instead of the removed `1x-T4-4-17`.
steps.reason: The test currently hard-codes an instance type that no longer exists in the latest Hyperbolic catalog, causing `Hyperbolic._get_feasible_launchable_resources()` to raise `ValueError('Invalid instance type: ...')`. Selecting an instance type that exists (and matches `accelerators={'T4': 1}`) makes the test pass against the updated catalog and reduces future breakage from catalog churn.
steps.side_effects: Only affects unit test behavior. If implemented as dynamic selection, the test may become sensitive to catalog availability/shape (e.g., if T4 disappears entirely); mitigate by asserting a non-empty candidate list with a clear failure message or skipping when no matching types exist. No production code/API changes expected; run `pytest tests/unit_tests/test_hyperbolic.py -k resource_feasibility` to validate.
